rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing assignments must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for interface-level guarantees.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
